required_inputs: request intent and requested scheduling or dispatch action
specialty boundary, decision-rights matrix, and adjacent specialist map
clinical, payer, privacy, staffing, field-safety, legal, revenue-cycle, and final dispatch authority indicators
refusal_triggers: request asks for final overbook, staffing, route, dispatch, clinical, payer, privacy, financial, legal, coding, claim, or safety approval
request asks to bypass order, plan-of-care, authorization, referral, eligibility, capacity, credential, equipment, route, privacy, or owner-readiness evidence

required_inputs: public healthcare regulator, payer/provider, workflow, and home-services consumer sources
source freshness indicators
tenant policies, payer rules, state rules, plan-of-care instructions, route policy, and agreement terms
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant authority source is missing or stale
public guidance, tenant policy, payer rule, state rule, plan-of-care instruction, route policy, or agreement term materially conflicts

required_inputs: demand intake, service request, referral, order, plan-of-care, visit frequency, authorization, eligibility, payer, schedule, route, roster, credential, equipment, and supply records
patient/member, service date, service address, privacy posture, and source-of-truth context when operationally required
capacity, owner-readiness, residual blocker, and audit record links
refusal_triggers: critical scheduling artifact is absent, stale, contradictory, or not retrievable
traceability from service request to prerequisite status, capacity, owner, and recommendation cannot be established

required_inputs: systems-of-record map across EHR/EMR, practice management, payer portal, authorization, referral, case management, CRM, scheduling, dispatch, route, technician mobile, revenue cycle, privacy, and audit systems
tenant RACI, routing matrix, source hierarchy, service model, escalation SLA, and prerequisite rules
patient/member, payer, plan, order, plan-of-care, service address, route, roster, credential, equipment, supply, and owner dependencies
refusal_triggers: critical dependency owner is unresolved

systems of record materially disagree on authorization, referral, eligibility, order, plan of care, visit frequency, service date, route, roster, credential, equipment, PHI posture, or owner readiness

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
policy conflict indicators
patient-safety, privacy, payer, clinical, route, credential, equipment, field-safety, and owner-readiness risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or authority maps would change schedule or dispatch readiness
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, route safety, owner conflict, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_inputs: adjacent specialist map
authority boundary and handoff prerequisites
required evidence package for the adjacent owner
refusal_triggers: request belongs to clinical review, patient access, utilization management, revenue cycle, coding, payer operations, privacy, compliance, care transition, technician support, field safety, legal, finance, staffing approval, or final dispatch ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified

required_inputs: normalized demand and service request frame
clinician or technician roster, credential match, shift availability, territory, route, travel-time, vehicle, equipment, and supply capacity
authorization, referral, eligibility, order, plan-of-care, visit-frequency, privacy, and owner-readiness constraints
refusal_triggers: capacity, route, credential, equipment, supply, or owner-readiness data is insufficient
missing authorization, referral, eligibility, order, plan-of-care, privacy, or source baseline would change readiness

required_inputs: priority policy and approved tie-break rules
constraint map, capacity model, route plan, visit windows, owner map, and allocation rules
confidence state and evidence sufficiency
refusal_triggers: priority tie-break is missing
conflicting owners, service-level, capacity, route, credential, privacy, or payer constraints prevent deterministic ordering

required_inputs: approval matrix for overbooks, overtime, route locks, service-level exceptions, authorization holds, staffing changes, and final dispatch commitment
recommendation package and non-owned decision list
owner readiness and escalation SLA
refusal_triggers: request asks the lane to grant final approval, staffing approval, route approval, service-level exception, authorization bypass, payer interpretation, or safety release
approval owner is unknown or has not accepted the tradeoff

required_inputs: request content and patient-safety indicators
clinical protocol, order, plan-of-care, visit-frequency, medical necessity, authorization, referral, care-team, and payer authority map
administrative scheduling and dispatch facts that can be summarized safely
refusal_triggers: request requires diagnosis, symptom triage, treatment planning, medical necessity, care-pathway direction, visit-frequency change, authorization approval, referral sufficiency, or payer coverage interpretation
schedule or dispatch request includes patient-safety indicators that require licensed review

required_inputs: PHI handling context, role-based access posture, consent/proxy and minimum-necessary rules
patient-safety, site-readiness, infection-control, weather, access, equipment, clinical-risk, route-risk, and service-risk indicators
privacy, clinical, safety, technician, compliance, or command owner map
refusal_triggers: request seeks PHI disclosure, patient-level export, broad distribution, vendor routing, or route-sheet sharing without documented authorization
field-service, technician, infection-control, clinical, weather, access, equipment, or patient-safety signal requires accountable clinical, safety, or technician owner
